items.title: U.S. Says It Struck 16 Iranian Mine-Laying Vessels Near Oil Route
items.summary: Whether any mines had been laid in the Strait of Hormuz since the war began on Feb. 28 is unclear. The Pentagon said 140 American service members had been wounded, eight severely, in the war.

items.title: New Supreme Leader Inherits Sprawling, Secretive Office That Dominates Iran
items.summary: His father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, had turned what was traditionally a religious affairs office into a shadowy national security juggernaut.

items.title: Civilians Killed by Strikes in Gulf States Are Almost All Migrant Workers
items.summary: Since the American-Israeli attack on Iran began, at least 12 civilians have been killed in oil-rich Gulf countries. All but one of them were foreign nationals.

items.title: Who Is Balendra Shah, the Rapper Set to Be Nepal’s Next Prime Minister?
items.summary: Balendra Shah’s party won a landslide in the election that followed Nepal’s Gen Z revolution. His style is pugnacious.

items.title: The Killing Of Haiti’s President Heads to a U.S. Court. What to Know.
items.summary: Four men are accused in South Florida of plotting to kill President Jovenel Moise in 2021. His death plunged Haiti into chaos that continues today.

items.title: Mother of Tumbler Ridge Shooting Victim Sues OpenAI
items.summary: The company banned the shooter’s ChatGPT account but did not alert the authorities, a move that amounted to fatal negligence, the family claims.

items.title: Cost-Cutting Led to South Korean Airport’s Deadly Wall, Report Finds
items.summary: The concrete runway barrier played a key role in a disaster that killed 179 people. An audit revealed officials skimped on construction fees and then falsified records.

items.title: Shooting at U.S. Consulate in Toronto a National Security Incident, Police Say
items.summary: The police are searching for two men who both fired at the building with a single handgun and fled in a white SUV.

items.title: Fear and Hope for Iranians Trapped Between Bombs and Defiant Rulers
items.summary: Many in Iran feel helpless in the face of their entrenched system, and some are becoming increasingly embittered by the fierce American and Israeli bombardment.

items.title: On the Road With Zelensky, Weathered, Weary and Fighting On
items.summary: Ukraine’s leader, Volodymyr Zelensky, traveled east to visit frontline troops trying to stave off Russian attacks, and invited reporters for The New York Times to go with him.

items.title: Ukraine Helps U.S. Bases in the Mideast With Stopping Drones
items.summary: As the war in Iran spreads, Kyiv is eagerly offering its hard-won expertise and advanced technology to counter Iranian drones.

items.title: Australia Grants Humanitarian Visas to 5 Members of Iranian Women’s Soccer Team
items.summary: Concern for the safety of the players had grown after Iranian state media criticized them for not singing the national anthem at a game in Australia.

items.title: Is the Trial of Erdogan’s Top Rival More About Corruption or Politics?
items.summary: Prosecutors accuse the former Istanbul mayor Ekrem Imamoglu of leading a criminal scheme. His supporters say Turkey’s president is trying to eliminate a political foe.

items.title: Trial of Ekrem Imamoglu, Erdogan’s Rival, Begins in Turkey
items.summary: Ekrem Imamoglu, the former mayor of Istanbul who stands accused of corruption, clashed with the judge and criticized the trial as baseless.

items.title: Trump Casts a Shadow Over One of Mexico’s Deadliest States
items.summary: President Trump wants to strike cartels inside Mexico. In Sinaloa State, a cartel stronghold, some residents said they were willing to entertain U.S. intervention.

items.title: A Dublin Pub-Crawl, but Hold the Booze
items.summary: A wander through some famed watering holes in Ireland’s capital reveals a booming alcohol-free scene and a cultural shift.

items.title: Here’s the latest.
This is what happened on March 10.
Iran’s New Supreme Leader Was Wounded Early in the War, Iranian and Israeli Officials Say
items.summary: Officials say Mojtaba Khamenei’s legs were hurt, but the circumstances as well as the extent of his injuries were unclear. He has remained out of view since being announced as leader three days ago.

items.title: Ukraine Can Now Manufacture ‘China-Free’ Drones
items.summary: The country has prioritized self-sufficiency in producing a crucial battlefield weapon, though weaning itself fully off cheaper Chinese components is difficult.
